prompt: quero criar algo para empresas organizarem melhor o trabalho delas, mas ainda não sei se será mais interno ou mais voltado para clientes

decisions: Public assets should be delivered through a CDN.
Use SSR or ISR for SEO-sensitive public pages when applicable.
Application UI and APIs must support locale-aware text, formatting, and translation resources.
Automation workflows require a background worker and durable job execution strategy.
Use SSR or ISR for SEO-sensitive pages.
Authentication is handled through secure sessions or JWT-based flows.
Public-facing pages should use caching and delivery strategies appropriate for anonymous traffic.
SEO-sensitive public pages should use rendering strategies such as SSR or ISR when beneficial.
Work items should support due dates, deadline validation, and overdue detection.
Work items should support explicit status progression and progress visibility.
Introduce caching for frequently accessed public content.

stories.title: Configure CDN
stories.description: Configure CDN for public site asset delivery.

stories.title: Add application locale support
stories.description: Add translation resources and locale-aware formatting to the internal application UI.

stories.title: Implement locale selection
stories.description: Allow users to choose locale and ensure locale-aware rendering and formatting.

stories.title: Setup job worker
stories.description: Create worker process responsible for automation jobs and scheduled workflows.

stories.title: Implement scheduled automation
stories.description: Implement scheduled jobs and automated workflows required by the application.

stories.title: Initialize project repository
stories.description: Create project structure using the selected stack.

stories.title: Setup database
stories.description: Configure postgres database and connection layer.

stories.title: Implement authentication
stories.description: Add login, logout and session management.

stories.title: Build internal dashboard shell
stories.description: Create the main internal application shell and navigation for team workflows.

stories.title: Model deadlines and due dates
stories.description: Implement domain support for deadlines, due dates, and related validations.

stories.title: Implement progress tracking
stories.description: Add progress tracking and status transitions for core work items.

stories.title: Setup monitoring and logging
stories.description: Integrate monitoring, logging and error tracking.

stories.title: Implement backups
stories.description: Automate database backups and retention policies.

answers.summary: Internal work organization tool, focused on deadlines, progress-tracking, with multilingual support and scheduled automation.

assumptions: The app will have a public-facing site component based on surface 'admin_plus_public_site'.
The app currently targets a generic web app shape but could evolve based on audience and feature needs.
Primary audience is internal teams but with some public-facing content or access.
Core work organization features focus on deadlines and progress tracking.
Internationalization is required to support multiple languages.
Scheduled or automated background jobs are needed for operational tasks.
open_questions: What is the primary audience for the tool (internal teams, external clients, or mixed)?
Will a CMS be needed to manage public site content?
Which core feature sets are required to organize work effectively?
Are scheduled jobs or background automation tasks necessary?
What specific public-facing content or features must be supported on the public site?
Are multiple user roles with varied permissions required?
Should the tool integrate with external services or APIs?
deployment_considerations: Docker deployment suitable for scalable service hosting.
Consider support for running scheduled jobs if required.
Public site components may require CDN or caching layers for performance.
Deploy target is Docker; ensure container orchestration readiness for scaling background jobs.
Plan for internationalization support in both backend and frontend layers.
No CMS needed so public content management likely manual or via other tools.
conflicts: Follow-up signals indicate an internal work organizer rather than a CMS-oriented product.

applied_answer_updates.summary: Internal work organization tool, focused on deadlines, progress-tracking, with multilingual support and scheduled automation.

needs_cms.question: Does the project need content management capabilities for managing and editing content easily?

needs_i18n.question: Does the application need to support multiple languages or internationalization?

needs_scheduled_jobs.question: Will the application require scheduled or background jobs (e.g. notifications, batch processing)?

performance: Public pages should render quickly under normal load.
API responses should target low median latency for public traffic.
Public assets should be cacheable and efficiently delivered.
scalability: System should support horizontal scaling of application servers.
Database must support concurrent authenticated users.
Background job workers must scale independently from interactive application traffic.
security: All authentication flows must enforce secure password hashing.
All external APIs must require authentication or signed requests.
Internal operational data must be isolated to authorized users and teams.
operational: Localization resources and locale handling must remain consistent across frontend and backend.
Structured logging should be used for all backend services.
Application must run in containerized environments.

philosophy: Favor workflow clarity and fast task-oriented navigation.
Optimize for dense, readable operational interfaces.
Reduce cognitive load for recurring internal workflows.
patterns: Use clear status states and visible progress markers.
Keep common actions close to work-item context.
Use optimistic UI carefully for low-risk edits.
Design text containers to handle locale expansion gracefully.
Ensure locale switching is easy to find and clearly reflected in the UI.
recommendations: Use Tailwind CSS or CSS variables for the token system.
Maintain a shared component library where possible.
Document component usage and accessibility expectations.

risks.title: Traffic spikes
risks.impact: The application may become unavailable or slow during peak traffic.
risks.mitigation: Use caching, CDN delivery, and capacity planning for anonymous traffic.

risks.title: Background job reliability
risks.risk: Automation depends on reliable job execution.
risks.impact: Important workflows may not run at the intended time.
risks.mitigation: Use a durable queue or reliable job persistence strategy.

risks.title: Clock drift
risks.risk: Worker and database clocks may diverge.
risks.impact: Scheduled tasks may run too early or too late.
risks.mitigation: Use UTC timestamps and centralized scheduling logic.

risks.title: Localization consistency
risks.risk: Translations and locale-specific formatting may drift across the application.
risks.impact: Users may experience inconsistent language, formatting, or fallback behavior.
risks.mitigation: Define locale ownership, fallback rules, and validation for translation resources.

risks.title: Workflow sprawl
risks.risk: Internal workflow requirements may expand rapidly after first adoption.
risks.impact: The product can become hard to maintain and difficult for teams to use consistently.
risks.mitigation: Keep the MVP focused and validate each workflow expansion with real usage.

risks.title: Status model complexity
risks.risk: Work-item states and transitions may become inconsistent over time.
risks.impact: Progress tracking and operational reporting become unreliable.
risks.mitigation: Define a clear status model and transition rules early.
